qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default 3600-second header timeout enables Slowloris-style denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns Tornado HTTPServer/HTTP1Connection slow header and body timeout behavior, while the advisory concerns synchronous parsing of multipart/form-data bodies with too many parts due to missing multipart part-count and complexity limits.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable multipart/form-data parser root cause. The only final qualifying finding is a distinct Slowloris-style timeout/resource-holding issue in Tornado's HTTP server connection handling.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-qjxf-f2mg-c6mc describes denial of service from synchronous parsing of multipart/form-data bodies with many parts before Tornado 6.5.5, where max_body_size was the only effective limit and ParseMultipartConfig-style part limits were absent.

slowloris_header_timeout_dos/vulnerability_report.json identifies tornado/httpserver.py header_timeout=idle_connection_timeout or 3600, body_timeout=None, and HTTP1Connection slow header/body reads as the vulnerable behavior.
The session input manifest lists slowloris_header_timeout_dos as the only final qualifying vulnerability folder.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding exists, so runtime tiering and PoC execution are not applicable.
